records.ety: [OF. conquest, conqueste, F. conqute, LL. conquistum, conquista, prop. p. p. from L. conquirere. See Conquer.]

defs: The act or process of conquering, or acquiring by force; the act of overcoming or subduing opposition by force, whether physical or moral; subjection; subjugation; victory.
quotes: In joys of conquest he resigns his breath.
Three years sufficed for the conquest of the country.

defs: That which is conquered; possession gained by force, physical or moral.
quotes: Wherefore rejoice? What conquest brings he home?

defs: The acquiring of property by other means than by inheritance; acquisition.
The act of gaining or regaining by successful struggle; as, the conquest of liberty or peace.
